Tests check overflowY, overflowX, hover rules, scrollbar properties, and column width — all discoverable from the instruction's description of the time panel column styling, overflow behavior, and CSS pseudo-selector interaction with touch events. The linting tests (biome, eslint) are pass-to-pass checks on the same fil

no_solution_leakage.reason: The instruction describes the symptoms (touch scrolling doesn't work, works after tap, related to CSS pseudo-selectors and touch events) but does not explicitly state to change overflowY from 'hidden' to 'auto' or remove the hover override. A developer must reason about the fix.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: The tests are quite specific about the exact implementation: test_overflow_y_default_is_auto requires overflowY to be 'auto' (not 'scroll' which would also fix touch scrolling), and test_no_hover_overflow_override requires the hover block to have NO overflowY at all (rather than just not having overflowY:'auto', or having it set to something else). An alternative fix using overflowY:'scroll' instead of 'auto' would fail test_overflow_y_default_is_auto even though it would fix the touch scrolling

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: The core tests (test_overflow_y_default_is_auto, test_no_hover_overflow_override, test_overflow_x_still_hidden, test_style_scrollbar_properties_intact, test_time_column_width_preserved) all use a Node.js script that reads the file content and extracts text blocks, then use regex to check string patterns. They do NOT execute the component, render it, or test actual scrolling behavior. The Node script just reads and parses text — it's equivalent to `assert 'foo' in open(path).read()` with extra st

test_not_tautological.reason: The unpatched code has overflowY:'hidden' as default with overflowY:'auto' in a hover block. test_overflow_y_default_is_auto would fail on the original code since the default is 'hidden', and test_no_hover_overflow_override would fail since the hover block exists. A stub/no-op would not pass these tests.

anti_cheating_measures.reason: The solution directory exists at /workspace/antd with solve.sh containing the exact patch. An agent could read /solution/solve.sh (if accessible) or the test file itself at tests/test_outputs.py which explicitly states 'Changing overflowY from hidden to auto' and 'Removing the hover-based overflowY override' in its docstring. The Dockerfile doesn't remove these files.

behavior_in_tests.reason: The instruction describes touch scroll not working due to CSS overflow/pseudo-selector interaction. The tests verify the overflow behavior change (overflowY auto, no hover override) and regression checks (overflowX, scrollbar styles, column width). All described behaviors are tested.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are well-named (test_overflow_y_default_is_auto, test_no_hover_overflow_override, etc.), each has a detailed docstring explaining purpose, and they're logically grouped by concern.

structured_data_schema.reason: This task involves CSS-in-JS styling changes, not structured data output.
